data.description:

data.title: Jack Dorsey questions Tether’s decision to donate only $250K to Bitcoin devs
data.description:
Twitter co-founder Jack Dorsey has criticized Tether’s decision to donate only $250,000 to Bitcoin developers. This week, the stablecoin giant announced a donation to OpenSats to support Bitcoin developers and Bitcoin-related projects. The fund was donated to boost the charity’s operations, which is expected to include the provision of grants to projects that support Bitcoin. […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/jack-dorsey-questions-tethers-250k-btc/

data.title: BlackRock dumped over $500 million of these cryptocurrencies this week
data.description:
This week’s cryptocurrency market sell-off has been reflected in the broader exchange-traded fund (ETF) sector, with BlackRock among the entities experiencing significant outflows. Specifically, the asset manager’s Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) spot ETFs collectively recorded a net outflow of approximately $524 million for the week ending October 17, 2025. The BlackRock iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) led the retreat, with roughly $279 million in outflows during the week. The heaviest withdrawals occurred on October 17, when IBIT shed about $268
source: https://finbold.com/blackrock-dumped-over-500-million-of-these-cryptocurrencies-this-week/

data.title: Coinbase Launches USDC Stablecoin Enterprise Payment Tool, Supporting Instant Global Settlement and Low-Fees Transactions
data.description: Coinbase announced that its Enterprise Services division has launched two new features—Global Payouts and Payment Links—that allow businesses to make payments and payments in the USDC stablecoin, enabling instant settlement, low transaction fees, and frictionless cross-border payments. Coinbase stated that traditional business-to-business (B2B) payments are plagued by high fees, slow settlement times, and cross-border friction. The new tools aim to streamline supplier payments, eliminate the risk of chargebacks, and provide API integration to support automated batch payments. Businesses can send USDC from their platform balance or linked bank account, and counterparties can receive the funds directly or create a Coinbase account via email. Additionally, merchants can generate shareable USDC payment links, allowing customers to pay instantly using wallets like Base, MetaMask, or Phantom, with no network fees. USDC balances in business accounts will automatically contribute to the USDC rewards program, currently earning a 4.1% annualized yield, and can be withdrawn to bank accounts at any time. Coinbase claims this solution provides a low-cost, efficient, and compliant digital payment solution for businesses worldwide. (crowdfundinsider)

data.title: Bitcoin's Potential Rebound Amid Gold's Pullback
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Bitcoin (BTC) may be on the verge of a significant rebound as gold (XAU) experiences a pullback. Analysts suggest that Bitcoin could rally to between $150,000 and $165,000 by the end of the year, based on technical analysis. The cryptocurrency is showing signs of bottoming out as gold's rally appears to have stalled after reaching an all-time high of approximately $4,380 per ounce. Despite a 2.90% drop since then, gold remains up by over 62.25% year-to-date.

Bitcoin has gained nearly 4% during gold's correction, recovering from its lowest point in four months near $103,535. Its relative strength index (RSI) is at its lowest since April, indicating a potential bottom structure similar to previous rebounds of 60% or more. This inverse relationship between Bitcoin and gold suggests that Bitcoin's price may be bottoming, according to some analysts. Analyst Pat highlights a "generational bottom" for Bitcoin, noting its performance relative to gold over the past four years. The Bitcoin-to-gold ratio has dropped to levels historically associated with market bottoms, last seen in 2015, 2018, 2020, and 2022, each followed by significant Bitcoin rallies.

Analyst Alex Wacy draws parallels between gold's current pullback and its 2020 peak, which coincided with a local Bitcoin bottom. The question remains whether gold will once again signal a bullish reversal for Bitcoin. Meanwhile, HSBC maintains a bullish outlook for gold, projecting it could reach $5,000 per ounce by 2026, driven by geopolitical tensions, economic uncertainty, and a weaker US dollar. The bank anticipates that long-term investors seeking portfolio stability will drive this rally, rather than short-term speculation.

Bitcoin's outlook remains optimistic, with JPMorgan analysts predicting it could reach $165,000 in 2025, arguing it is undervalued relative to gold. Analyst Charles Edwards also suggests that a breakout above $120,000 could propel Bitcoin toward $150,000 rapidly. This article does not provide investment advice or recommendations. Readers should conduct their own research before making investment decisions.

data.title: White House Economic Advisor to Address AI Developments
data.description: According to PANews, White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett announced plans to deliver a speech on artificial intelligence within the next one to two weeks. U.S. President Donald Trump continues to seek an amicable resolution to the government shutdown issue. The shutdown is expected to halt nearly all government operations, with the exception of defense activities.
